preview_text:
The model favors Senegal with a 60% win probability, giving the team a commanding edge over Iraq's 13% chance. The 47-percentage-point gap reflects a substantial disparity in the forecast, though the model assigns non-trivial probability to an upset.

The spread suggests a moderately competitive match despite Senegal's advantage. A draw carries meaningful probability at 27%, indicating the model sees realistic paths to a stalemate. Iraq's baseline win chance, while slim, is not negligible; the match is unlikely to be one-sided in execution, even if the underlying quality gap is wide.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both shift weight toward Senegal compared to the model—by roughly 7 percentage points on the win outcome. All three sources agree on Iraq's floor near 12%, but the market is more confident in a Senegal victory and less confident in a draw. The divergence is modest but consistent; the dashboard makes no claim as to which assessment is more accurate.
